<!-- Auto-generated by `pnpm changeset version` at release time. Do not edit directly â€” see CONTRIBUTING.md Â§ Changesets to add an entry via `pnpm changeset`. -->

## 1.1.0

### Minor Changes

- #1901 `bc6204f` Thanks @alafemina_sfemu! - Fail the build when an extension's `target-config.json` references a UITarget ID that does not exist in the application source code. Also removes an invalid store-locator footer target entry that this validation caught.

- #1932 `35d961b` Thanks @mjuraschik_sfemu! - w:

    Embedded Content Blocks (ECB)

    Adds support for fetching and rendering Page Designer components outside
    of a page context. The header is wired up as the first embedded component,
    hosting an `announcement` region for the new announcement banner. Data
    collection for embedded components reuses the same `componentData` promise
    map that pages use, so streaming and per-component Suspense boundaries
    work the same way for both.

- #2132 `7af1edb` Thanks @j-sheth_sfemu! - Fix the dev hybrid proxy to handle every `url.prefix` shape out of the box, so a locale-prefixed legacy path (e.g. `/uk/cart`) is no longer double-stacked into `/s/{siteId}/{locale}/uk/cart`.

    The dev proxy unconditionally prepended `/s/{defaultSiteId}/{locale}` to every non-SFRA path. Now that the template emits prefixed paths (`/uk/cart`, `/global/en-GB/cart`), that stacked the locale a second time and SFRA 404'd. The proxy now reads `app.url.prefix` and the site/locale alias catalog from `config.server.ts`, strips the prefix the URL already carries, and reuses that site/locale when decorating to SFRA's `/s/{siteId}/{locale}/â€¦` shape â€” for no prefix, `/:localeId`, and `/:siteId/:localeId` alike. A captured segment is only treated as a site/locale when it is a known identifier, so a bare legacy path (`/cart` under `/:localeId`) isn't mistaken for a locale home. The root `/` maps to the locale-less SFRA site root and the query string is preserved across the rewrite.

    `hybridProxyPlugin` gains optional `urlPrefix`, `siteAliases`, `localeAliases`, and a last-resort `rewritePath` escape hatch for non-standard URL models. `@salesforce/storefront-next-runtime` exports a new `extractPrefixParamValues` site-context helper (mirrors `stripPathPrefix`'s matching rules) that powers the prefix-aware rewrite.

- #2162 `588f000` Thanks @daniel-diaz_sfemu! - `create-storefront` can now generate from a vertical template. The interactive prompt offers the Retail (Fashion) and Beauty (Cosmetic) storefronts alongside the custom-URL option, and a new `--vertical`/`-V` flag (`fashion` | `cosmetic`) selects one non-interactively. With `--defaults` and no template or vertical specified, the Fashion storefront is used. An explicit `--template` still takes precedence over `--vertical`.

- #1937 `6c57057` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Auto-discover extension config files. An extension can now ship a `src/extensions/<name>/config.ts` that default-exports its client-side config defaults, and the framework discovers it at build time, merges it into `config.app.extension.<camelCaseFolder>`, and derives the TypeScript type automatically â€” no edits to `config.server.ts` or `src/types/config.ts`. Merchants override per environment with `PUBLIC__app__extension__<key>__<setting>`. Adds the `sfnext config aggregate-extensions` CLI prestep (run by `pnpm dev`, `pnpm build`, and `pnpm typecheck`), mirroring the existing extension locale aggregation.

- #2096 `89c07df` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Upgrade react-router and @react-router/\* to 7.18.0. Patches an unauthenticated RCE plus four other advisories in the vendored turbo-stream dependency.

    **ACTION REQUIRED (security):** This advisory is only patched once your project resolves `react-router` / `@react-router/*` at `>= 7.18.0`. The SDK declares react-router as a peer dependency, so the version is pinned by your own `package.json`, not by the SDK â€” bumping `@salesforce/storefront-next-*` alone does not pull the fix. Bump react-router and `@react-router/*` to `7.18.0` in your project's `package.json`. Projects generated fresh from the template already get `7.18.0`.

    This release also renames React Router APIs that stabilized in 7.15 (dropping their `unstable_` prefix). `unstable_optimizeDeps` stays unstable and is unchanged.

    **ACTION REQUIRED â€” if you ejected `entry.server.tsx` and export your own instrumentations:** React Router 7.18 reads the server-entry export named `instrumentations`, not `unstable_instrumentations`. The framework matches this export by name, so the old name still compiles â€” it just silently stops registering your instrumentation. Rename `export const unstable_instrumentations` to `export const instrumentations`.

    The remaining renames surface as `pnpm typecheck` errors, so they're caught at build time: `unstable_ServerInstrumentation` â†’ `ServerInstrumentation` (type), route arg `unstable_pattern` â†’ `pattern`, `future.unstable_subResourceIntegrity` â†’ top-level `subResourceIntegrity`, `future.unstable_trailingSlashAwareDataRequests` â†’ `future.v8_trailingSlashAwareDataRequests`, `future.unstable_passThroughRequests` â†’ `future.v8_passThroughRequests`.

    Loader/action/middleware arg _types_ now include a `url: URL` field (RR 7.18 type change). Runtime behavior is unchanged â€” the framework still provides URLs via `request.url`; only types and test fixtures needed updating. No production loader/action change is required.

- #1964 `4fe15ff` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Auto-discover server-only extension config. Parallel to the existing `config.ts` aggregator, an extension can now ship a `src/extensions/<name>/server-config.ts` that default-exports its server-only defaults â€” vendor SCAPI overrides, retry budgets, internal endpoints â€” and the framework merges them into `config.app.serverExtension.<camelCaseFolder>`, reachable via `getConfig(context).serverExtension.<key>` from server loaders, actions, and middleware. Values never reach the browser: the root loader strips `app.serverExtension` before React Router serializes the SSR hydration payload, and a Vite plugin fails the build if any client chunk imports the generated barrel. The existing `sfnext config aggregate-extensions` CLI now writes both barrels (`index.ts` and `server.ts`) in one run via `Promise.all` â€” no second command, no second prestep. There is intentionally no `PUBLIC__` override path â€” the same AST validator forbids `process.env`, so true secrets must continue to be read from `process.env` in a server route.

### Patch Changes

- #1894 `ebae987` Thanks @daniel-diaz_sfemu! - Fix `/resource/recommendations` returning HTTP 403 on MRT by enabling Express `trust proxy` and using `resolveRequestOrigin` in the same-origin gate

- #2125 `ae04ec8` Thanks @kevin-he_sfemu! - Fix inbound distributed-trace continuation on Managed Runtime. The server span now continues the inbound W3C `traceparent` by parsing it and building the parent context with `trace.setSpanContext`, instead of `propagation.extract`. In the externalized MRT Lambda bundle the global W3C propagator registered at startup is not visible to the request middleware â€” a different `@opentelemetry/api` instance owns the global registry, so `propagation.fields()` is `[]` and `propagation.extract` returns ROOT â€” which made every request start a fresh root trace, splitting each request across two unlinked traces (inbound MRT trace vs. the storefront's). The context-key path used by `setSpanContext` is realm-global (`Symbol.for(...)`) and survives the split. Verified against live MRT logs (inbound and response trace IDs match, with a distinct child span).

- #2148 `e539822` Thanks @kevin-he_sfemu! - Connect server-side SCAPI and SLAS calls to the storefront's distributed trace on Managed Runtime: these outbound requests now carry the W3C `traceparent` header, so they appear on the same end-to-end trace as the inbound storefront request instead of as separate, disconnected traces.

    OpenTelemetry spans are now emitted under stable, low-cardinality operation names (`sfnext.request`, `sfnext.loader`, `sfnext.action`, `sfnext.fetch` for outbound requests, and the like) rather than names containing the request method, path, or URL, so traces aggregate cleanly and the storefront shows up as a single service in the trace topology. The request method, path, matched route, and outbound URL are recorded as span attributes instead. If you built dashboards or queries against the previous span names, point them at the new ones.

- #2113 `42c9d2d` Thanks @arayanavarro_sfemu! - Make the generated standalone storefront lint clean out of the box . Three generator outputs were emitted in a shape the project's own Prettier/ESLint then rewrote, so a freshly generated project failed `pnpm lint`:
    - `src/extensions/config.json` â€” `trimExtensions` now formats the written config with the consuming project's Prettier (resolved from the project, graceful fallback if absent) instead of raw `JSON.stringify(â€¦, null, 4)`, which multilined short arrays and omitted the trailing newline.
    - `eslint.config.js` â€” `generate-eslint-config.js` assembles the standalone config by string concatenation; it now runs the result through the project's Prettier before writing so the printWidth/indentation match the project's lint rules.
    - `e2e/eslint.config.mjs` â€” its base-config import is re-pointed from `../../../eslint.config.js` (monorepo layout) to `../eslint.config.js` (flat standalone layout), so `pnpm --filter ./e2e lint` no longer fails with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.

- #2130 `d5f9b0e` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Bump `undici` to `7.28.0` for security remediation. Exact-pinned via a workspace override plus a direct manifest bump in `@salesforce/storefront-next-dev`. The OpenTelemetry undici instrumentation hooks Node's global `fetch` (which is undici under the hood) for trace propagation; `undici` is also reached transitively via `@salesforce/b2c-tooling-sdk`.

- #2059 `2e7ec0f` Thanks @clavery_sfemu! - Upgrade `@salesforce/b2c-tooling-sdk` (1.11.0 â†’ 1.15.0) in `storefront-next-dev` and `@salesforce/b2c-cli` (1.12.0 â†’ 1.17.0) in the template. b2c-cli 1.17.0 depends on b2c-tooling-sdk 1.15.0, so both resolve to a single tooling-sdk version. The 1.15.0 release drops the `applicationinsights` dependency, substantially shrinking the transitive tree (removes the Azure Monitor / OpenTelemetry / gRPC packages pulled in transitively). No source changes â€” the sfnext CLI commands that extend the SDK's base commands (`push`, `config inspect`, `deploy-cartridge`) inherit the updated base behavior automatically.

- #2073 `b80d30d` Thanks @kevin-he_sfemu! - Participate in the B2C distributed trace via W3C Trace Context. When tracing is enabled (`SFNEXT_OTEL_ENABLED=true`), the storefront now continues the inbound `traceparent` (so its spans share the upstream MRT trace ID) and propagates `traceparent` on outbound SCAPI/backend fetches, using the standard OpenTelemetry `W3CTraceContextPropagator`. MRT's sampling decision is honored via a `ParentBasedSampler` â€” the storefront starts no competing trace for unsampled requests and implements no sampling policy of its own. No behavior change when tracing is disabled; the span log exporter and its output format are unchanged.

- #1970 `c55c95b` Thanks @arayanavarro_sfemu! - Loosen the `@salesforce/storefront-next-runtime` peer-dependency range from `workspace:*` to `workspace:^` (published as `^X.Y.Z` instead of an exact pin). Combined with enabling Changesets' `onlyUpdatePeerDependentsWhenOutOfRange`, this stops a routine `minor` runtime change from forcing a `major` SDK release. Customers installing the dev SDK now accept any compatible runtime within the same major, matching how the two packages are actually developed in lockstep.

- #2005 `9d7cf52` Thanks @j-sheth_sfemu! -@: Fix a non-fatal "Could not load config from config.server.ts: Cannot find module" build warning, and restore event-instrumentation validation that was silently skipped as a result. `loadEngagementConfig` (used by the event-instrumentation validator plugin) loaded `config.server.ts` via native `import()`, which cannot resolve TypeScript's extensionless relative imports (e.g. `./src/types/tracking-consent`) when the plugin runs in plain Node during a production build. The import threw `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`, the error was swallowed (the build still succeeds), and validation never ran. It now loads through the same jiti-based `importTypescript` helper used by every other config loader in the SDK.

- #2096 `89c07df` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - fix(mrt): keep streamed SSR responses flowing on the Managed Runtime adapter

    The Managed Runtime streaming adapter builds a response object whose `write()` returned the downstream stream's backpressure signal. React Router 7.16+ treats a falsy `write()` as a cue to await a `drain` event on the response before sending the next chunk, but the Managed Runtime response stream does not surface `drain` on that response object, so large streamed responses (such as a deferred-loader route flushing a second Suspense chunk) stalled until the request timed out. The adapter now reports each accepted chunk as written rather than relaying the underlying stream's backpressure, restoring the flow behavior React Router relied on before 7.16. Real flow control still happens inside the compression â†’ response-stream pipe.

- #1892 `75f6dc2` Thanks @vcua_sfemu! - Fix Managed Runtime returning HTTP 502 for any response that ends with no body. Previously, routes that returned `Response(null, { status: <any> })` â€” for example analytics beacon proxies, `204 No Content` responses, and 5xx error responses returned without a body â€” produced a generic `502 InternalServerErrorException` from API Gateway instead of the application's intended status code. Empty-body 3xx redirects already had a workaround; this extends it to all status codes.

    If your storefront uses the built-in Active Data analytics adapter, every `__Analytics-Start` beacon was failing in production with this 502, silently dropping analytics events. Recent Active Data dashboards may underreport â€” review with that in mind after deploying.

- #2241 `7dae22d` Thanks @khaberstock_sfemu! - Fix a `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'Agent')` that crashed the MRT SSR Lambda (`Runtime.ExitError`) on any request that instantiated an AWS SDK client (e.g. via `@salesforce/mrt-utilities` â†’ `@aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch` â†’ `@smithy/node-http-handler` â‰Ą 4.7.7).

    With `environments.ssr.resolve.noExternal: true`, the Managed Runtime bundle force-bundles dependencies that do `import { Agent } from 'node:https'`, which esbuild emits as `node_https.default.Agent`. When Rollup's CJS interop resolved to `esModule`, no `default` export was synthesized for the `require('node:https')` binding, so `node_https.default` was `undefined` at runtime. The SSR bundle now pins `build.rollupOptions.output.interop: 'auto'`, so Rollup synthesizes the `default` export and the access resolves.

- #2190 `54dde0a` Thanks @sprasoon_sfemu! - Internal release tooling â€” advance the 1.1.0 alpha window to `1.1.0-alpha.1`. The `1.1.0-alpha.0` SDK packages were already published during earlier CI workflow validation, and npm versions are immutable, so the first real `1.1.0` pre-release ships as `alpha.1`. No customer-facing or runtime impact.

- #2161 `93eb6f2` Thanks @daniel-diaz_sfemu! - Remove the UITarget development overlay and its smoke-test harness. These were internal-only debugging aids that could cause build failures when trimming extensions.

    The `uiTargetDevModePlugin` export from the SDK is now deprecated and returns a no-op plugin for backward compatibility. If your project imports this plugin, remove the `uiTargetDevMode()` call from your `vite.config.ts` and delete any `vite-plugins/ui-target-dev-mode.ts` wrapper file. The export will be removed in v2.

- #1900 `e38ea13` Thanks @j-sheth_sfemu! - When a strict CSP with nonces is in use, the SDK now propagates the request nonce to its internal bundle-config inline script (the SDK transparently substitutes the customer's `<Scripts>` import with its own wrapper at build time, so this happens automatically). No customer action required.

- #2055 `4343407` Thanks @rahul-kumawat_sfemu! - Surface non-blocking Managed Runtime deploy warnings (including the upcoming x86 environment deprecation notice) when running `sfnext push`. The warning is informational and does not block the push.

- #1869 `592b2b6` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Format the generated page-designer static registry with the project's Prettier config so it is no longer reformatted on every commit. Previously the generator emitted trailing whitespace and over-width `registerImporter` lines, so a standalone `prettier --write` rewrote the file on every commit. The generated file is now also exempted in the ESLint config alongside other generated artifacts, and the redundant `/* eslint-disable */` it used to emit is dropped.

## 1.0.1

### Patch Changes

- #1988 `871cf56` Thanks @vmarta_sfemu! - Remove pilot/beta service disclaimers and pilot-only documentation links from package READMEs now that Storefront Next is generally available at v1.0.0. The retail template README now points to the published developer docs at developer.salesforce.com instead of the gated pilot docs site.

- Updated dependencies [`871cf56`]:
    - @salesforce/storefront-next-runtime@1.0.1

## 1.0.0

### Minor Changes

- #1823 `0a3fcdc` Thanks @arayanavarro_sfemu! - w:

    Tighten the `@salesforce/storefront-next-runtime/config` public surface for V1 GA.

    **Removed from public exports** (followups to #1741):
    - `ConfigContext` â€” internal React context backing `useConfig()`. Read config via `useConfig()` instead.
    - `createAppConfig` â€” one-line `.app` accessor with no semantic value. Use `staticConfig.app` directly.
    - The `middleware.ts` source file and `createAppConfigMiddleware` factory were also deleted; they had B2C-Commerce-specific validation hardcoded in (`commerce.api.{clientId,organizationId,shortCode}`, `SCAPI_PROXY_HOST`) and the retail template ships its own validating middleware. Future templates write their own.

    **Added** â€” `AppConfigShape` interface as a module-augmentation hook for `getConfig()` / `useConfig()` typing. Each template `declare module`s once in its own types file and gets typed access without the per-call `<AppConfig>` generic:

    ```ts
    // In your template's src/types/config.ts:
    declare module '@salesforce/storefront-next-runtime/config' {
        interface AppConfigShape extends AppConfig {}
    }
    ```

    **Other changes**
    - `Locale`, `Site`, `Url` JSDoc reframed as opt-in baseline shapes â€” `BaseConfig<App>` is generic so future templates can ignore them.
    - `defineConfig` JSDoc now explicitly notes it reads `process.env` at call time (server-only side effect).
    - `mergeEnvConfig`'s engagement-specific protected-paths error message is now generic.
    - Retail-flavored examples in `schema.ts` / `utils.ts` JSDoc neutralized.
    - Template (`template-retail-rsc-app`): all 110+ `getConfig<AppConfig>()` / `useConfig<AppConfig>()` call sites now use the augmented hook (no per-call generic).
    - Template `commerce.sites: Site[]` now imports the richer `Site` shape from `@salesforce/storefront-next-runtime/site-context` (reconciles the duplicate `Site` types that existed across both subpaths).
    - `contact-info.tsx` migrated from `useContext(ConfigContext)` to `useConfig()` (the only production caller of the bypass pattern).
    - README-CONFIG.md fixed: `loadConfig` subpath was wrong (`/load-config` â†’ `/config/load-config`); `appConfigContext` now documented for custom middleware composition; `AppConfigShape` augmentation pattern documented.

    Public surface delta: 13 runtime + 5 type exports â†’ 5 + 6 (plus `loadConfig` at the separate `./config/load-config` subpath).

    > **Semver note for future maintainers:** This entry is a `minor` bump _only because the package is still pre-V1 GA_. Removing public exports is a `major` once V1 ships â€” do not paste this pattern after lock-in.

- #1511 `4c9db91` Thanks @alafemina_sfemu! - Add action hooks Vite plugin: generates `virtual:action-hooks` module from extension `target-config.json` registrations, enabling server-side extension points in checkout actions

- #1541 `ddbfb84` Thanks @kevin-he_sfemu! - Add `baseConfigPlugin` that contributes framework-wide Vite defaults (React/React Router dedupe, pre-bundled React Router entries) so these don't have to live in the template's `vite.config.ts`

- #1324 `b84fbc0` Thanks @unandyala_sfemu! - Add `enabled` boolean field to `target-config.json` component entries: components with `enabled: false` are skipped at build time and tree-shaken from the production bundle; entries without the field default to `true` (backward compatible)

- #1744 `2f00128` Thanks @clavery_sfemu! - Add `@salesforce/storefront-next-runtime` as a peer dependency so the CLI can read the runtime's `BUILT_IN_CLIENT_DEFAULTS` map directly â€” keeping the per-client config (basePath, locale-awareness) in sync with the SDK by construction

- #1744 `2f00128` Thanks @clavery_sfemu! - Add `sfnext scapi add` support for overriding built-in SCAPI shopper clients (e.g. `shopperProducts`) in addition to adding net-new custom APIs. Override-vs-custom is detected automatically from the resolved client key; overrides inherit the SDK's per-client locale-awareness and base path so they behave identically to the SDK client they replace. Also adds `sfnext scapi available` to survey the active tenant and report which built-in shopper APIs have `c_*` custom attributes worth overriding

- #1727 `a441c16` Thanks @dbarbato_sfemu! - Make `allowedActionOrigins` generic: read `SFW_FUNCTIONAL_DOMAIN` env var instead of hardcoding the functional domain, and add Pomerium reverse proxy origin pattern

- #1738 `5535ca2` Thanks @kevin-he_sfemu! - Remove internal exports from the main package entry point (`actionHooksPlugin`, `extractPatterns`, `testPatterns`, `clearCache`, `createServer`, `loadProjectConfig`, `loadConfigFromEnv`, `trimExtensions`, `generateMetadata`) to reduce the public API surface for GA; these are either composed internally by `storefrontNextTargets` or available via dedicated subpath exports

- #1322 `60c6fbb` Thanks @clavery_sfemu! - Add `editorDefinition` support to the `@AttributeDefinition` decorator for Page Designer components. Custom attribute editors (e.g. `einstein.globalrecommenderselector`) can now be declared on a decorated attribute and are emitted as `editor_definition` in the generated cartridge JSON. Per the SFCC schema, `editor_definition` is only valid on attributes of type `custom`.

- #1945 `2241fb2` Thanks @vmarta_sfemu! - Adopt a two-track versioning model: the SDK packages stay on SemVer (locked together), while the template moves to a CalVer date stamp (e.g. "June 2026"). Generated projects now record their origin in `package.json` under a namespaced `storefrontNext` block (`templateRelease`, `templateVersion`, `minSdkVersion`), so a customer can always tell which template release they generated from. `create-storefront` surfaces the template release in its completion banner and links the new compatibility matrix (`docs/COMPATIBILITY.md`), which maps each template stamp to the SDK version it needs.

### Patch Changes

- #1923 `46b1ceb` Thanks @daniel-diaz_sfemu! - Fix `/resource/recommendations` returning HTTP 403 on MRT by enabling Express `trust proxy` and using `resolveRequestOrigin` in the same-origin gate

- #1837 `ece6a58` Thanks @j-sheth_sfemu! - Bump `handlebars` from 4.7.8 to 4.7.9 in `storefront-next-dev` and add pnpm overrides for `@isaacs/brace-expansion@^5.0.1`, `axios@^1.15.2`, `basic-ftp@^5.3.1`, `flatted@^3.4.2`, and `shell-quote@^1.8.4`. Overrides live in both `pnpm-workspace.yaml` (protects this monorepo's dev/CI) and `template-retail-rsc-app`'s `pnpm.overrides` (so customer-generated projects pick them up since pnpm only honors overrides at the workspace root). Addresses 10 of 11 critical CVEs from a pre-GA Snyk scan; none are exploitable in the shipped runtime â€” every vulnerable package is reachable only via dev/build tooling â€” but the duplication ensures customer projects get clean Snyk output on day one.

- #1848 `26a7f95` Thanks @clavery_sfemu! - Bump `@salesforce/b2c-tooling-sdk` to `^1.11.0` and `@salesforce/b2c-cli` to `^1.12.0` (latest published versions). Scope the oclif `init` hook to sfnext commands only so it no-ops cleanly when the package is loaded as a plugin under a host CLI (e.g. `b2c sfnext`); standalone `sfnext` behavior is unchanged.

- #1939 `d273772` Thanks @arayanavarro_sfemu! - Fix three dev-server console errors in freshly generated projects (`pnpm dev`):
    - **Invalid hook call / "Cannot read properties of null (reading 'useContext')"**: on first dev load Vite discovered the React-importing runtime SDK entry points (`/config`, `/security/react`, `/site-context`, `/design/react/core`, `/routing/app-wrapper`, `/i18n/client`) lazily at request time, triggering a dep re-optimization + full reload that transiently loaded a second React instance. Also pre-bundles the i18n peer deps the SDK imports internally but the template does not import from its own source (`i18next-browser-languagedetector`, used by `/i18n/client`; and `remix-i18next/middleware`, used by the SDK's `/i18n` barrel) â€” these are discovered late for the same reason. (`react-i18next` needs no entry: the template imports it directly, so Vite's initial source crawl already finds it.) All are now in the first-pass `optimizeDeps.include`, so a single optimization runs with one shared React and no mid-session reload.
    - **CSP blocked the Vite HMR websocket** (`ws://localhost:24678`): the security middleware now appends `ws://localhost:*`, `ws://127.0.0.1:*`, and `wss://localhost:*` to `connect-src` only when running locally (`BUNDLE_ID` unset or `local`). Deployed/MRT responses are unchanged.
    - **Nonce hydration mismatch** on the inline `window.__APP_CONFIG__` script: browsers strip the `nonce` content attribute from the DOM after applying CSP, so the client saw `nonce=""` against the server's real value. Added `suppressHydrationWarning` to that script element; the CSP nonce still applies.

- #1921 `9adf311` Thanks @vcua_sfemu! - Fix Managed Runtime returning HTTP 502 for any response that ends with no body. Previously, routes that returned `Response(null, { status: <any> })` â€” for example analytics beacon proxies, `204 No Content` responses, and 5xx error responses returned without a body â€” produced a generic `502 InternalServerErrorException` from API Gateway instead of the application's intended status code. Empty-body 3xx redirects already had a workaround; this extends it to all status codes.

    If your storefront uses the built-in Active Data analytics adapter, every `__Analytics-Start` beacon was failing in production with this 502, silently dropping analytics events. Recent Active Data dashboards may underreport â€” review with that in mind after deploying.

- #1751 `65223d9` Thanks @j-sheth_sfemu! - Fix the hybrid-proxy Vite plugin dropping the original request's query params when SFCC redirects to a canonical SFRA URL (e.g. `/cart` â†’ `/s/{siteId}/{locale}/Cart-Show`). The proxy now merges the request's query string into the redirect target, with the redirect target winning on key collisions and multi-value keys (e.g. `pmid=PROMO1&pmid=PROMO2`) preserved on both sides. Local-development only â€” MRT/eCDN deployments are unaffected.

- #1831 `2fb0e1f` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Fix the hybrid proxy plugin so SFRA's `plugin_redirect` cartridge actually redirects in local development. `plugin_redirect` sometimes returns HTTP 200 with a `Location` header instead of a proper 3xx redirect; browsers only follow `Location` on 3xx responses, so the proxied page rendered blank. The `proxyRes` handler now detects this case (status 200 + non-empty `Location`), converts it to a 302 with the localhost-rewritten `Location`, drops the upstream body, and preserves `Set-Cookie` so session continuity is maintained. Affects local development only â€” production routing is handled by Cloudflare eCDN and is unaffected.

- #1702 `4003e3a` Thanks @jie-dai_sfemu! - Drop stale `SFDC_EXT_THEME_SWITCHER` references after the theme-switcher extension was removed from the template: replace fixtures in `extensibility/dependency-utils.test.ts` and `extensibility/target-utils.test.ts` with `SFDC_EXT_MULTISHIP`/`multiship`, and refresh CLI examples in `extensions install`, `extensions remove`, and the package README to use `SFDC_EXT_BOPIS`

- #1540 `5c55ab8` Thanks @kevin-he_sfemu! - Fix dev-only `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'currency')` (or similar) in loaders that consume site context, caused by Vite externalizing the SDK for some import sites and transforming it for others â€” producing two `dist/site-context.js` module records and two `createContext()` keys. `baseConfigPlugin` now sets `ssr.noExternal: ['@salesforce/storefront-next-runtime']` so all SSR import sites resolve through Vite's transform cache and share one module record. Production builds were never affected (`managedRuntimeBundlePlugin` already inlines the SDK). Reverts the relevant part of #1540.

- #1611 `2012a3c` Thanks @kevin-he_sfemu! - Fix `managedRuntimeBundle` plugin build error when a CSS file references a `public/` or bundled asset (e.g. `@font-face { src: url(...) }`). `renderBuiltUrl` now returns `{ relative: true }` for `hostType === 'css'` instead of the JS runtime expression Vite rejects inside CSS `url()`. The browser resolves the relative path against the stylesheet's own URL â€” which already carries the per-deploy `BUNDLE_ID` â€” so the reference stays correct across deploys

- #1741 `9152bc4` Thanks @kevin-he_sfemu! - Mark `Scripts` component (`./react-router/Scripts`) as private internal API â€” it is automatically applied via the `patchReactRouter` Vite plugin and should not be imported directly

- #1678 `a54506a` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Rename the configuration labels that the `sfnext create-storefront` command asks for when run against the bundled retail template, to align with Commerce Cloud documentation: `SLAS Client ID` (formerly `API Client ID`), `Organization ID` (formerly `API Organization ID`), `Short Code` (formerly `API Short Code`), `Site ID` (formerly `Default Site ID`). Labels are sourced from the template's `config-meta.json`.

- #1744 `2f00128` Thanks @clavery_sfemu! - The `sfnext scapi` codegen now emits a template-local `src/scapi/index.ts` barrel that wildcards the runtime SCAPI surface and shadows overridden namespaces with locally-generated wrappers; `sfnext scapi list` groups output into Overrides and Custom APIs sections; `sfnext scapi remove` cleans up the per-override namespace wrapper alongside the schema/types/operations files

- #1727 `a441c16` Thanks @dbarbato_sfemu! - Validate `allowedActionOrigins` in `reactRouterConfigResolved` to prevent overrides in workspace environments

- #1511 `4c9db91` Thanks @alafemina_sfemu! - Warn at build time when multiple UITarget components or action hook handlers share the same target ID and order value (non-deterministic execution order)

- #1907 `af03e94` Thanks @j-sheth_sfemu! - When a strict CSP with nonces is in use, the SDK now propagates the request nonce to its internal bundle-config inline script (the SDK transparently substitutes the customer's `<Scripts>` import with its own wrapper at build time, so this happens automatically). No customer action required.

- Updated dependencies [`b370405`, `f7e9554`, `0a3fcdc`, `d355c50`, `8ae7030`, `3535909`, `d273772`, `dee3c0a`, `d39ccce`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `ba93c10`, `0d500de`, `3c9c3e5`]:
    - @salesforce/storefront-next-runtime@1.0.0

## v0.4.0 (May 5, 2026)

- Move `pnpm-workspace.yaml.hbs â†’ .yaml` conversion from `create-storefront` CLI into mirror sync scripts; `prepare-standalone-template.js` now pins exact versions in `minimumReleaseAgeExclude` for all exempted packages
- Extract i18n locale chunking into SDK Vite plugin (`i18nPlugin`): splits translation files into per-language chunks automatically
- Add `sfnext locales aggregate-extensions` CLI command: generates per-locale barrel files aggregating extension translations under `extPascalCase` namespaces
- Remove local data-store provider export/build artifacts and rely on `@salesforce/mrt-utilities` data-store behavior (#1533)
- Update `sfnext dev` to forward Node `--conditions` (from `process.execArgv` and `NODE_OPTIONS`) into Vite client + SSR resolution for conditional exports (#1533)
- Fix HMR cascade in static registry plugin: skip unnecessary file writes and module reloads when registry content is unchanged
- Add `dw.json` to `.gitignore` to prevent credentials from being accidentally committed
- Replace `dotenv` dependency with Node built-in `util.parseEnv` and `process.loadEnvFile`; consolidate `.env` loading into the oclif `init` hook
- Add `sfnext config inspect` command: shows a `config.server.ts` override summary â€” which values are overridden by `.env` and MRT `PUBLIC__` vars
- Remove unused /callback route
- Enable `future.unstable_optimizeDeps` in React Router preset to fix duplicate React module crash on dev server startup
- Add local dev support to MRT Data Layer #1215
- Clean up RSC/React Server Components references from documentation and comments (#1363)
- Fix incorrect SSR sourcemaps for files sharing a basename in local dev debugger
- Remove internal "odyssey" codename references from codebase
- Add `sfnext scapi` CLI used to support generating and managing custom API clients in template projects
- Fix `generate-cartridge` to scan `config-metadata/` for aspect type definitions (pdp.json, plp.json) after recent move from `src/`
- Align `generate-cartridge` and static registry with template Page Designer defaults: `storefrontnext_base` default group, Layout/Content component folders and type ids

## v0.3.1 (Apr 13, 2026)

- Simplify `create-storefront` workspace yaml generation: copy `pnpm-workspace.yaml.hbs` directly from the template instead of using Handlebars templating and directory-scanning
- `prepare-standalone-template.js` now pins SDK package versions in `minimumReleaseAgeExclude` entries of `pnpm-workspace.yaml.hbs` at release time, so generated storefronts exempt exactly the published version from the quarantine window
